Layer 2 or Layer 3 injection on UNIX-like systems
IP and TCP options from file

arguments.description: Target host, IP, MAC, or URL the tool operates on.

arguments.description: Target port (default: service default).

arguments.name: --interface
arguments.description: Network interface to bind to (e.g. wlan0mon, eth0).

arguments.name: --output

arguments.name: --verbose
arguments.description: Increase log verbosity (repeatable for higher levels).

arguments.name: --threads
arguments.description: Number of concurrent workers.

arguments.description: Per-operation timeout in seconds.

arguments.name: --rate
arguments.description: Packet / request rate limit (packets/sec).

arguments.description: Path to a wordlist (users, passwords, paths).

arguments.name: --proxy
arguments.description: HTTP or SOCKS proxy URL for outbound requests.

arguments.description: HTTP User-Agent header value.

arguments.name: --cookie
arguments.description: HTTP Cookie header value.

arguments.name: --header
arguments.description: Extra HTTP header in key:value form (repeatable).

function_signatures.description: Index entry in the Wireless category. Use as a candidate tool in the chain planner after a NVD/lookup pass on the target vendor.

function_signatures.name: pairs_with_the_wireless_system_prompt_stanza_in_core_ai_backend_chain_to_teach_the_llm_the_standard_invocation_pattern
function_signatures.description: Pairs with the Wireless system-prompt stanza in core.ai_backend.chain to teach the LLM the standard invocation pattern.

function_signatures.name: operator_initiated_the_chain_step_is_gated_by_the_per_step_accept_cancel_gate_the_tool_does_not_auto_execute
function_signatures.description: Operator-initiated: the chain step is gated by the per-step ACCEPT/CANCEL gate; the tool does not auto-execute.

function_signatures.description: Use as a teaching example in the prompt stanza so the LLM can reason about the right run order.

chain_examples.note: Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with t6x/reaver-wps-fork-t6x (score=11); pair in the chain planner.
Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with RuhanSA079/WiFiPacketRadio (score=11); pair in the chain planner.
Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with unknown/scrcpy (score=10); pair in the chain planner.

Phase 3: same attack_surface=['wifi_2_4_ghz', 'wifi_5_ghz', 'shell_linux'] and phase_hint='any'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.

Phase 4: same attack_surface=['wifi_2_4_ghz', 'wifi_5_ghz', 'shell_linux'] and phase_hint='any'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.

Phase 5: same attack_surface=['wifi_2_4_ghz', 'wifi_5_ghz', 'shell_linux'] and phase_hint='any'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.

documentation.how_to_use: Install/setup: git clone https://github.com/libnet/nemesis.git. Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `Nemesis >= 1.5 require libnet 1.1, or later.`. trust.warning: Attribution/index entry only. Audit provenance, code, releases and licence before use.

use_cases: When you need Wireless, use nemesis because N E M E S I S - Crafting & Injection ====================================== [![License Badge][]][License] [![GitHub Status][]][GitHub] [![Coverity Status][]][Co.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `Nemesis >= 1.5 require libnet 1.1, or later.` as documented upstream for nemesis.
When chaining Wireless tools, pair nemesis with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `nemesis --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
